characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The title character and series protagonist. A timid and mild-mannered wallaby who freshly immigrated from Australia to live in the USA. All-Loving Hero: InStatic Cling, he was the most resistant to change aside from Ed Bighead (with the plot to save O-Town being him clinging to the past, and wanting the Fatheads back on the air).That said, unlike Ed, when Rachel Bighead was revealed to have transitioned, he was instantly accepting, alongside Heffer and Filbert, being the compassionate wallaby that he is. In fact, when Ed comes around to accept Rachel’s identity, it’s ultimately what convinces Rocko to embrace the present. All There in the Manual: His last name, from the book"Not Just Cartoons: Nicktoons!"is Rama.(as in, Rock-o Rama.Get it?)This is not mentioned anywhere in the series (due to the staff not reaching a consensus, he was left without one proper during the show), similar to the protagonist ofanother Nickelodeon show.The comics, meanwhile, give his full name as Rockefeller Coinsworth Wallaby. The comics, meanwhile, give his full name as Rockefeller Coinsworth Wallaby. Animal Gender-Bender: Averted. Being a male wallaby he clearly lacks a pouch.

characters.name: Rocko Rama
characters.description: The title character and series protagonist. A timid and mild-mannered wallaby who freshly immigrated from Australia to live in the USA. All-Loving Hero: InStatic Cling, he was the most resistant to change aside from Ed Bighead (with the plot to save O-Town being him clinging to the past, and wanting the Fatheads back on the air).That said, unlike Ed, when Rachel Bighead was revealed to have transitioned, he was instantly accepting, alongside Heffer and Filbert, being the compassionate wallaby that he is. In fact, when Ed comes around to accept Rachel’s identity, it’s ultimately what convinces Rocko to embrace the present. All There in the Manual: His last name, from the book"Not Just Cartoons: Nicktoons!"is Rama.(as in, Rock-o Rama.Get it?)This is not mentioned anywhere in the series (due to the staff not reaching a consensus, he was left without one proper during the show), similar to the protagonist ofanother Nickelodeon show.The comics, meanwhile, give his full name as Rockefeller Coinsworth Wallaby. The comics, meanwhile, give his full name as Rockefeller Coinsworth Wallaby. Animal Gender-Bender: Averted. Being a male wallaby he clearly lacks a pouch.

characters.name: Spunky
characters.description: Rocko's pet bull terrier. All Dogs Are Purebred: Can trace his lineage all the way back to the Stone Age, and he appears to be a bull terrier. Artistic License – Biology: Considering dogs usually live up to a maximum of 15 years, Spunky should be dead by the timeStatic Clingtakes place. In fact, if he really did grew up with Rocko, he wouldn't be young and kicking by the time Rocko is an adult. Canine Companion: To Rocko.Depending on the Writer, he either grew up with Rocko, or was found by him as an adult. Cargo Ship:In-Universe, he falls in love with a mop, and when "she" gets stolen by anExpyof Sigmund Freud, Spunky finds love again... with a hydrant.

characters.name: Heffer Wolfe
characters.description: Rocko's best friend. A gluttonous, simple-minded cow..erm,steerliterallyRaised by Wolves. Acrofatic: He's pretty nimble for a fat guy, for he can skate surprisingly well, as shown in the episode "Wallaby On Wheels". Adopt the Food: He was raised bya family of wolveswho had intended to fatten him up and eat him (according to Peter, George even used to call him "steak"), but they came to love him as a son and passed off the markings for the various cuts of beef on his body as a "birthmark". Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: He's a yellow steer with green hair. Ascended to Carnivorism: He's a normally herbivorous animal who loves to eat meat (and anything else too). Justified by the fact he was raised by carnivores.

characters.name: Filburt Shellbach
characters.description: Rocko's other best friend. A geeky turtle with numerous phobias. Early on, he was more of a generic character. Filburt got married to Dr. Hutchison in the third season, becoming a father in season 4. Ambiguously Jewish: His last name is Shellbach, apart from being partly an homage toWoody Allen. He also uses the Yiddish word "schmutz" in one issue of the comics. Ascended Extra: He started off as aRecurring Extra, but before Season 1 was over, he was fleshed out as Rocko's other best friend. Babies Ever After: He and Dr. Hutchison end up having children. Beware the Nice Ones: While Filburt is fairly friendly, pushing him too far isn't a good idea.

characters.name: Edward "Ed" Bighead
characters.description: Rocko's cantankerous toad neighbor who hates Rocko (and everything else). Works as a corporate drone for Conglom-O Corporation. Angrish: He often mutters incoherently under his breath whenever he's greatly annoyed. Asshole Victim: Whenever heTakes a Level In Jerkass, he will get his just desserts for it. The episodes "Canned" and "Zanzibar" are blatant examples. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Despite all of the dysfunction between him and Bev, several of their character centric-episodes end with an enforcement of this trope. Bad Boss:In "Canned". How many people hire someone just tokillthem, anyway?In "Gutter Balls", he's shown to be an abusive bowling team manager. After shouting at Virginia Wolfe for being a terrible bowler ("YOU BOWL LIKE A WIMP!!"), Virginialeaves in mid-tournament and goes home, and teammates Tammy and Bev follow suit. Leaving Ed with no bowlers left and an attempt to cheat by disguising himself as Bev (the tournament disallows substituting players in, including the non-bowling team captain), whichdoesn't work.

characters.name: Beverly "Bev" Bighead
characters.description: Ed Bighead's wife. Much nicer than her husband is, though she can still be a grouch on occasion. Alliterative Name: Her first and last names both begin with B. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: She has this moment with Ed a lot of times. Brooding Boy, Gentle Girl: The considerate gentle girl to her husband's brooding boy. Characterization Marches On: In the first season, there were frequent jokes surrounding her status as aDirty Old Woman, to the point of attempting to cheat on her husband with a much younger man (Rocko) when she thinks he doesn't love her anymore. This got heavily toned down later on and she became more of an affable housewife character.

characters.name: Earl
characters.description: A terrifying and sadistic bulldog that plagues O-Town. Later in the show he ends up being adopted by Mrs. Bighead. Advertised Extra: Though he appears in the opening of every episode, chasing Rocko, Earl only makes sporadic appearances throughout the series. Angry Guard Dog: He becomes one to Bev Bighead after she adopts him. Unfortunately his preferred target is her husband Ed, which Bev is oblivious to. Animal Testing: His second appearance has him starting out imprisoned at Dr. HP Marten's Institute for Questionable Experiments before being broken out by his Fairy Dogmother. One of the reasons Bev adopts him is after realizing he was being experimented on. Bully Bulldog: Earl is a rapid bulldog who takes obvious joy in mauling anything or anyone he can sink his claws or teeth into.

characters.name: Dr. Paula "Hutch" Hutchison
characters.description: An energetic cat dentist with a hook hand who's almost never seen without a big smile on her face. Became Filburt's wife in season three. Affirmative Action Girl: Was put into the show because the higher-ups wanted more female characters. Babies Ever After: She ends up having children with Filburt. Breakout Character: Despite Joe Murray’s reluctance to add a decent female character and only appearing in one episode in season one, Hutch proved to be quite a hit with the staff, the fanbase, and Murray himself. Come season two, she became Filburt’s love interest and later, his wife. Catchphrase: "Kay?" (often with the sound of her neck straining)

characters.name: Gilbert, Shellbert, Norbert, and Missy
characters.description: Filburt and Dr. Hutchison's four children, introduced at the end of "From Here to Maternity". A Day in the Limelight: "Future Shlock", the series'Distant Finale, is an episode that focuses primarily on them, as Gilbert and Norbert finda seventeen-year-old bananain Rocko's refrigerator, and when they show it to Filburt, Filburt tells them, Shellbert, and Missy the story of the last time he saw Rocko and Heffer before they were blasted into space. Family Theme Naming: Gilbert, Shellbert, and Norbert all have names that end in "Bert". Gender Equals Breed: Gilbert and Shellbert are turtles like their father, while Missy is a cat like her mother. Norbert, on the other hand, is a steer, since Heffer incubated their egg. Odd Name Out: Missy does not have a name that ends in "Bert" like her brothers.

characters.name: George Wolfe
characters.description: Heffer's easily irritated but loving father. Berserk Button: While Heffer only gets mildly annoyed at worst when called a cow instead of a steer, George will usually yell whenever Heffer is called a cow. Hair-Trigger Temper: George is very easily pissed off, especially regarding Heffer and Peter. Happily Married: With Virginia. George can get annoyed with his wife, but he ultimately does love her and feeling is undoubtedly mutual. Jerkass Has a Point: Yes, he is pretty strict and hot-tempered towards his peers, but you honestly can't blame him for being frustrated withHeffer being babied by Virginiain spite of his grown-up age in Mama's Boy.

characters.name: Virginia Wolfe
characters.description: Heffer's kind but overly coddling and repressed mother. Beware the Nice Ones: She's an incredibly sweet and good-natured woman who shows the most open affection and acceptance of her children. However, like the rest of her family, she does eat fellow sapient animals with it being played forBlack Comedy. Character Tics: Her nervous twitch, which acts up whenever her family does something dysfunctional despite Virginia herself never dropping her kindly demeanor. Drives Like Crazy: The episode "Driving Mrs. Wolfe" reveals that she's a truly terrible driver, so she takes lessons from Rocko so her husband will allow her to drive the new family car. Good Parents: Smothering tendencies aside, Virginia really is a supportive, loving mother to all her children.

characters.name: Peter Wolfe
characters.description: Heffer's snarky brother. Ambiguously Gay: Peter hasn't been outright stated to be gay, but it'sveryheavily implied. Either that or he is aWholesome Crossdresser. Deadpan Snarker: Peter is one of the snarkiest characters on the entire show. Neet: Peter is a high-school dropout who doesn't seem to have a job. The Stoic: Peter is extremely deadpan.

characters.name: Cindy Wolfe
characters.description: Heffer's melodramatic sister. Out of Focus: Compared to the other members of the Wolfe family, Cindy gets the least lines and the least character focus. Talking with Signs: In "An Elk for Heffer", Cindy is stated to have taken a "vow of silence" and communicates this way.

characters.name: Hiram Wolfe
characters.description: Heffer's incredibly rude grandfather, who's prejudiced against wallabies and beavers, mistaking Rocko for the latter. Gasshole: Hiram's lines are frequently punctuated with a loud fart sound effect. Grumpy Old Man: Hiram Wolfe is Heffer's grandfather who never seems happy and is always shown complaining or insulting "the beaver" (Rocko). Jerk with a Heart of Gold: In "Cruisin", Rocko falls overboard the boat amidst the chaos.Since Hiram had been de-aged into young adulthood, he immediately tried to save him, nevermind that he usually seems to despise Rocko. Posthumous Character: Somewhere in-between the series proper and the Static Cling special, Hiram died and is seen as a ghost in his appearance.

characters.name: Chuck and Leon Chameleon
characters.description: A pair of snooty chameleons with exaggerated Swedish accents. Usually seen running random businesses. Always Camp: They're always in a career field associated with fashion or art. Ambiguously Gay: There is a lot of evidence to prove it in: "Camera Shy" they call rocko "Baby" and "Honey", in "The Big Question" at one point they are seen dancing together at the high school dance and in "Dumbells" they reveals that they live together in the same house. Catchphrase: "I love this job!" And also referring to their business as "exclusive", and also, "Tsk, tsk." Cloudcuckoolander: Even more so than Heffer.

characters.name: Really Really Big Man
characters.description: O-Town's local superhero. His power is the, ahem..."Nipples of the Future" which he stretches out into people's eyes and shows them the future. The Big Guy: Um, duh... The Cape: He's a superhero who fights for what is right. Cartoon Creature: He mostly looks like a green-skinned human, but with antennae. Seeing as he's a Superman expy, it's easy to assume he's an alien, but he's apparently a bug. Darker and Edgier: InStatic Clingwe see him starring in anexcessively dark and violent movieabout himself. Although he's shown to be the sameNice Guywe know and love outside of said movie.

characters.name: Mr. Dupette
characters.description: The head ofConglom-O Corporationand Ed Bighead's boss, although hestarted outas the owner of a comic book shop. Appears to be a reptile of some kind. Cartoon Creature: What is he exactly? An iguana? A crocodile? A dragon? A dinosaur? Character Tic: He's always picking his nose. Corrupt Corporate Executive: He's the head of Conglom-O and is often a very unpleasant person. Though this gets downplayed later on.Subverted in "Zanzibar!" where after Rocko and the mob invades his conference room. Unlike most depictions of a corporate executive inGreen Aesopepisodes, he's ultimately more negligent than genuinely malevolent. Subverted in "Zanzibar!" where after Rocko and the mob invades his conference room. Unlike most depictions of a corporate executive inGreen Aesopepisodes, he's ultimately more negligent than genuinely malevolent.

characters.name: Mr. Smitty
characters.description: Rocko's cruel boss at the Kind of a Lot o' Comics who often gives Rocko a hard time at work. He is the only character that Rocko openly dislikes. Bad Boss: It takes a special kind of jackass to leave his only employee in the comic store to fix a minor mistake onevery last comic cover all night!How Smitty hasn't gotten charged for this is an enigma. Bait-and-Switch Comment: He often says something genuinely complimentary to Rocko right before either switching back to chastising him for whatever mistake he makes or demands him to work on whatever he's ordered to do. Cigar Chomper: He is almost always seen smoking on a cigar. Fat Bastard: A fat, grumpy toad who even makesEd Bigheadlook far more sympathetic in comparison.

characters.name: Gladys, the Hippo Lady
characters.description: A large hippopotamus woman in stretch pants. Don't invade her personal space.That won't end well. Actually Pretty Funny: She completely goes from being mad, to laughing hysterically, when Rocko explains the concept of ding-dong-ditch. Angry, Angry Hippos: She has aHair-Trigger Temperand often beats up Rocko foraccidentally invading her personal space. Big Fun: Once Rocko gets to know her better, she enjoys having fun with him, being openly affectionate and dragging him on a ding dong ditch spree all over O-Town. Catchphrase:"How DAAAAARE you?!"

characters.name: Melba Toast
characters.description: All Love Is Unrequited: Doesn't seem to notice that Rocko exists, and is in a relationship with a man named David. The Faceless: The only part of Melba that we get to see is her legs. Girl Next Door: Literally. She's Rocko's other next door neighbor and he's in love with her. Leg Focus: Her gorgeous legs are often all we see of her. Love Interest: Rocko has a huge crush on her but she's never seen interacting with him and it's unclear if she even knows he exists.

characters.name: Peaches
characters.description: He may have a silly name, but he's probably the closest thing the show has to aBig Bad. Lives inYou-Know-Where. Affably Evil: He's in charge of sentencing souls to Heck, but can take time to amuse others with some jokes. Arch-Enemy: Peaches could be considered Heffer's since his focus episodes have him tormenting Heffer exclusively and trying to get his soul. Not that Heffer seems to be aware of him. Big Bad Wannabe: He succeeds in taking Heffer's soul but he gets bullied into giving him back by Heffer's grandmother. Big "NO!": Belts one out as being given "the Ultimate Punishment."

characters.name: Bloaty the Tick and Squirmy the Ringworm
characters.description: Two parasites that live on Spunky. Artistic License – Biology: Despite the name, ringworm infection is not caused by a parasitic worm, let alone one with a hoop-like anatomical structure. It's actually caused by a fungus. Additionally, Squirmy is claimed to be an intestinal parasite. Real ringworm is actually a skin condition. Expy: When initially introduced, they were parodies of Kramden and Norton fromThe Honeymooners. Fat and Skinny: Bloaty is the fat guy, Squirmy is the skinny guy. Show Within a Show: Whenever an episode focuses on them, a catchy theme-song pops up (or in the case of "Down the Hatch", a serious set of subtitles appears instead).

characters.name: Gordon the Foot
characters.description: An anthropomorphic human foot. Catchphrase: "See ya later, nits!" No Celebrities Were Harmed: His voice is an imitation ofJohnny Carson. Running Gag: Some of the ends of his sentences are often punctuated by an offscreen voice (imitating EdMcMahon, later revealed to be the Hopping Hessian) shouting, "Yyyyyes!!!" Those Two Guys: Very often seen alone, but in two episodes, he is this with either the Hopping Hessian or Judge Sockner.

characters.name: Captain Compost Heap
characters.description: Captain Compost Heap is a talking (and singing) pile of compost who appears in "Zanzibar". Remember the New Guy?: Heffer pretends that the Captain had always been there and even tries to make awkward small talk with this character he claims he's always known."So, what's up CCH?" Sudden Sequel Death Syndrome: He appears for a brief moment in "Static Cling" before he gets crushed by Rocko's house.
